campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025, to review the city's finances for July, including how the city invested its money and its overall financial health that month. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including different ways residents can get around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment transactions and money management
City treasurer's financial report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan (improving transit, biking, walking options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or law. After hearing public input, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the normal first reading step and fast-tracking it for a final vote at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on new ordinance
First reading waived to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Residents had opportunity to speak for or against the proposal

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered routine administrative approvals from previous meetings and moved forward on a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March meetings (regular, special, and closed sessions)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to organize upcoming council agendas. The council reviewed what items should be added to their April 21st and April 28th meetings, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked public records from early April.
keyTopics: Organizing April 21, 2026 final council agenda
Planning April 28, 2026 draft agenda items
Setting time-certain slots for scheduled discussions
Reviewing public records from April 2-9, 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee matters

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including budgeting for infrastructure projects like road repairs, parks, and public facilities. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: 2026-27 capital improvement budget and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition
City spending plans for public facilities and services

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting notes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The council introduced a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city code, which deals with protections for residential renters.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance amending residential tenant protections program
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code
Housing and human services policy updates

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also reminded people about lobbying registration rules—if you're trying to influence city decisions, you may need to officially register and report your activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget workshop and public engagement
Lobbying registration requirements under Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
Rules for disclosing attempts to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026, to discuss updates to the city's Charter (its basic governing rules). Chair Pat Nikolai joined the meeting remotely from Oregon. Residents could participate in person or online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings continuing
Remote participation option via Zoom for residents
Chair Pat Nikolai attending from Oregon
Written public comments accepted via email to cityattorney@santaclaraca.gov

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several routine business items. The main focus was reviewing a major update to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, considering changes to how council vacancies are filled, and reviewing requests from individual council members for new work projects.
keyTopics: Update to the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Change to the council vacancy ordinance (fewer positions or different filling process)
New work projects requested by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to upgrade the building's historic status and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space in exchange for renovating the building and making it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown Commercial zone development proposal
